title: cirsium horridulum f. elliottii
intro: Cirsium horridulum f. elliottii, also known as Elliott's thistle or prickly thistle, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Cirsium horridulum f. elliottii have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exerting antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions and digestive issues to respiratory problems and infections.
